immutable_core.semantic_definition: A one-row-per-hospital-admission encounter duration summary retaining elapsed days from the current hospital discharge boundary to the first later different hospitalization observed in the captured source system.
immutable_core.semantic_grain: one row per anchor-qualified summary target
immutable_core.same_name_cross_database_rule: Any database asset published as std_days_to_next_hospital_admission must preserve hospital-admission encounter grain, hospital discharge anchoring, and first-later-different-hospitalization semantics; ICU readmission timing, same-hospitalization ICU return timing, binary readmission flags, and all-system utilization claims require separate identities or separate approval.

summary_contract.window_start_rule: start at the current hospital discharge timestamp
summary_contract.window_end_rule: end at the first later different hospitalization admission timestamp observed for the same subject
summary_contract.qualifying_row_rule: one retained row per hospital admission encounter with subject_id, hadm_id, and discharge boundary present
summary_contract.selection_rule: within the same subject, select the first later different hospitalization whose admission timestamp is strictly after the current discharge timestamp
summary_contract.tie_break_rule: order candidate future hospitalizations by admission timestamp and hospital admission identifier after enforcing strict post-discharge timing and different-hospitalization rules
summary_contract.no_source_row_action: do not synthesize hospital admission rows outside the source admissions table; retain a null value for source admissions with no later different hospitalization observed
summary_contract.partial_window_action: fail or reopen the contract when the current discharge boundary is missing; do not reinterpret missing future events as zero duration

canonical_representation.range_role_note: The retained value is semantically nonnegative. Long observed gaps can occur because this is an observed future-utilization duration inside the captured source; the plausible band is a review cue, not an exclusion rule.

null_semantics.null_meaning: No later different hospitalization was observed after discharge in the captured MIMIC source snapshot.
null_does_not_mean: the patient was proven never to be rehospitalized
the patient had zero days to next admission
the patient was followed in all external hospital systems
null_semantics.status_column_policy: No separate status column is retained in the current MIMIC asset because the source audit found complete current discharge timestamps and the retained null has one intended meaning.

external_alignment.alignment_policy: Use external outcome and utilization terminology as semantic context only; govern this variable primarily by discharge anchor, future admission search rule, encounter grain, and day-unit representation.
external_alignment.note: This is a source-observed utilization duration, not a direct coded clinical event and not a binary readmission measure.

new_variable_trigger_examples: changing from hospital-admission encounter grain to ICU-stay grain
changing from time-to-next hospital admission to a binary 30-day readmission flag
changing from first later different hospitalization after discharge to same-hospitalization ICU return
claiming all-system readmission follow-up rather than source-observed follow-up inside the captured database
version_bump_trigger_examples: adding a new reviewed database mapping while preserving the same hospital-admission discharge anchor and future-admission search rule
adding a status column for a database with incomplete discharge boundaries while keeping the same retained duration semantics

allowed_database_specific_variation: local identifiers used to normalize patient and hospital-admission encounter keys
database-specific names for hospital admission and discharge timestamps
database-specific evidence proving whether future admissions are source-observed only or more complete within a health system
database-specific status handling when discharge timestamps are missing, if explicitly mapped
disallowed_same_name_variation: publishing ICU readmission or next ICU admission timing as std_days_to_next_hospital_admission
publishing a 30-day readmission binary indicator as this duration variable
treating null retained values as zero days
claiming complete all-system rehospitalization follow-up without database-specific proof
using ICU or ICU/MC admission boundaries as hospital admission/discharge boundaries without a separately proven hospital-level bridge

execution_governance.formal_output_rule: Formal std_days_to_next_hospital_admission outputs should go through execution.py reading this variable_spec and an approved mapping_spec. Execute-mode runtime evidence should record both spec hashes and formal output-file signatures.
execution_governance.future_non_bypassable_rule: Formal std_days_to_next_hospital_admission outputs should be generated only through the governed execution.py path reading this variable_spec and an approved mapping_spec.

not_yet_approved_database_notes: AmsterdamUMCdb-1.0.2 is not approved for same-name publication in this opening step. It first needs a separate hospital-admission encounter bridge proving true hospital admission and discharge boundaries rather than ICU/MC stay boundaries.
